$comment: flowmap:mutate corpus — each entry is one deliberate defect the suite MUST react to as `expect` says. Seeded from the AUD3 mutation spot-check (docs/flowmap/audit/03-tests.md §2): M1 and M3 SURVIVED then; the AUD5 fixes (F-02, F-04) added the tests that kill them, and this harness keeps that kill PROVEN, not remembered. `expect` states CURRENT reality, measured — a mismatch in either direction fails the harness (a caught->survived flip is a lost gate; a survived->caught flip means update the corpus). `find` must occur exactly once in `file` or the harness refuses (stale corpus, exit 2). `fast` is the tier-1 command; tier-2 (--tier full) always runs the whole spec:test:all.

mutations.origin: AUD3 M1 (timestamp deny) retired when F4 became content-truth; now guards the H5 content-falsifiability deny — the gate's live deny path
AUD3 M2a — masked mutant pre-AUD5 (downstream deny compensated); F-01 pinned the primary branch by reason text
AUD3 M2b — CAUGHT pre-AUD5 (the one deny the gate had was locked)
AUD3 M3 — SURVIVED pre-AUD5; killed by F-04's roadmap deny suite
mutations.find: return { pass: size >= min, auto: true, why:
mutations.replace: return { pass: true, auto: true, why:
